topic: Establishing optimal governance mechanisms for artificial general intelligence development and deployment

teams.perspective_description: Create comprehensive domestic regulatory frameworks via specialized federal AI oversight agency with testing, approval, and monitoring authority.
evidence_preferences: regulatory precedent from pharma and finance
teams.counterargument_strategy: Demonstrate regulatory capacity and show that good regulation can enhance innovation.
teams.viewpoint_orientation: Strongly supports federal AI oversight agency with safety testing, market approval, and monitoring.
typical_arguments: Regulatory success stories in other sectors
Institutional capabilities to align AI with public interest

members.name: Dr. Patricia Regulation-Foster
education: PhD in Public Policy (University of Chicago)
experience: 22 years in technology regulation and administrative law
Legal advisory at FCC on technology policy
Professor of Technology Law at Georgetown Law
members.current_role: Professor of Technology Law, Georgetown University Law Center

members.name: Professor James Security-Thompson
education: PhD in Political Science (Stanford) - security policy
experience: 19 years in national security technology assessment
Professor of National Security Studies at Naval War College
members.current_role: Professor of National Security Studies, Naval War College
members.typical_counterargument_approach: highlight strategic risks and propose control mechanisms

members.name: Dr. Susan Consumer-Kim
education: PhD in Public Policy (UC Berkeley) - consumer policy
experience: 16 years in consumer protection and tech impact assessment
FTC advisory on technology and privacy
Director of Technology Policy at Consumer Reports
members.current_role: Director of Technology Policy, Consumer Reports
members.risk_tolerance: moderate for beneficial tech, high aversion to harm
members.typical_counterargument_approach: center user risks and propose enforceable safeguards

teams.perspective_description: Governance via standards bodies, private safety initiatives, and market incentives that preserve innovation speed.
evidence_preferences: comparative outcomes of market vs regulation
teams.counterargument_strategy: Show incentive alignment with safety and highlight regulatory ossification risks.
teams.viewpoint_orientation: Strongly supports industry-led governance via standards organizations and voluntary initiatives.
typical_arguments: Incentive compatibility between safety and markets

members.name: Dr. Michael Innovation-Chen
experience: 18 years in AI systems development and tech leadership
Engineering leadership at major AI companies
Advisory to venture firms on AI tech assessment
CTO at leading AI development company
members.risk_tolerance: high for advancement, moderate for safety
members.typical_counterargument_approach: demonstrate how private incentives already address safety

members.name: Professor Sarah Market-Rodriguez
education: PhD in Economics (University of Chicago) - industrial organization
experience: 15 years in technology economics and market structure
Advisory to tech companies on market strategy
Economic consulting on antitrust and innovation policy
Professor of Technology Economics at Stanford GSB
members.current_role: Professor of Technology Economics, Stanford GSB
members.typical_counterargument_approach: show distortion risks of regulation and benefits of competition

members.name: Dr. David Standards-Wilson
experience: 20 years in standards development and industry governance
Leadership in major technology standards bodies
Advisory to industry associations on self-regulation
Director of Industry Standards at IEEE
members.typical_counterargument_approach: show self-regulation effectiveness and rapid iteration

teams.perspective_description: Coordinate global AI development through binding international agreements and shared oversight institutions.
teams.counterargument_strategy: Demonstrate necessity of cooperation and preserve sovereignty via well-designed treaties.
teams.viewpoint_orientation: Supports international AI governance organization with binding authority for safety, sharing, and coordinated response.

members.name: Dr. Elena International-Petrov
experience: 21 years in international law and technology governance
UN specialized agency advisory on technology governance
Professor of International Technology Law at Fletcher
members.current_role: Professor of International Technology Law, Fletcher School
members.typical_counterargument_approach: design treaty mechanisms that preserve sovereignty while coordinating

members.name: Professor Ahmad Development-Hassan
experience: 17 years in technology diffusion and development policy
World Bank advisory on technology and development
Consultation for developing country governments on tech policy
Professor of International Development at SAIS
members.current_role: Professor of International Development, Johns Hopkins SAIS
members.typical_counterargument_approach: propose capability-building and equitable access provisions

members.name: Dr. Lisa Cooperation-Garcia
experience: 19 years in international organization leadership and multilateral coordination
Management roles in major international organizations
Director of Global Governance Studies at CFR
members.current_role: Director of Global Governance Studies, Council on Foreign Relations
members.argumentation_preference: coordination case studies and institutional design
members.typical_counterargument_approach: show coordination mechanisms and shared responsibility
members.industry_background: international organizations and policy think tanks

custom_listeners.name: Dr. Elizabeth Warren-Smith
experience: Professional practice in health policy, economics

custom_listeners.name: Professor James Law-Wilson
experience: Professional practice in law, civil liberties

custom_listeners.name: Dr. Maria Healthcare-Rodriguez
experience: Professional practice in medicine, patient care

custom_listeners.name: Brenda Real-Estate-Thompson
experience: Professional practice in real estate, markets

custom_listeners.name: Tina Pet-Rodriguez
experience: Professional practice in animal welfare, care
